domain:
A Person has a name and an identifier. Every Person is the child of another Person and can have an indeterminate number of children (however, there will be one Person who is not the child of any other Person). A Person has an indeterminate number of ancestors (the ancestors of a Person are their father and the ancestors of their father). We will be interested in knowing the degree of each ancestor of a Person (1 for the father, 2 for the father's father, etc.).

A Resource has a name. There are two types of Resources, such as Files and Folders. Every resource belongs to a Folder and cannot exist outside of a Folder (however, there will be one Folder that does not follow that rule, the root Folder does not belong to any other Folder). Each Folder contains an indeterminate number of Resources.

A User is a Person who, in addition to a name and an identifier, also has a password to access an indeterminate number of Resources, and each Resource can be accessed by an indeterminate number of Users. When a User can access a resource, we will be interested in knowing if they can view it and if they can edit it.

questions.question:
Is ancestor degree a relevant domain concept that should be included in the model?

For example:
1. Ancestor degree is a relevant concept in this domain and should be represented in the model.
2. Ancestor degree does not correspond to any relevant domain concept and should be removed from the model.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Keep ancestor degree, it is a relevant domain concept.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Remove ancestor degree, it is not relevant.
questions.selected: Option 2: Remove ancestor degree, it is not relevant.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of User in the relationship with Resource?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a resource can exist without any associated user.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a resource must have at least one associated user.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Resource can exist without User.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Resource must have at least one User.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Resource can exist without User.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of User in the relationship with Resource?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a resource is associated with exactly one user.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a resource can be associated with multiple users.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each resource links to one user.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each resource can link to multiple users.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each resource can link to multiple users.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Resource in the relationship with User?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a user can exist without any associated resource.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a user must have at least one associated resource.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, User can exist without Resource.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, User must have at least one Resource.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, User can exist without Resource.

questions.question:
What is the maximum cardinality of Resource in the relationship with User?

For example:
1. Maximum cardinality of one, a user is associated with exactly one resource.
2. Maximum cardinality of many, a user can be associated with multiple resources.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: One as maximun cardinality, each user links to one resource.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each user can link to multiple resources.
questions.selected: Option 2: Many as maximun cardinality, each user can link to multiple resources.
